seoTitle: How to Buy Microsoft Excel and Use Promo Codes
metaDescription: Buy a genuine Microsoft Excel license through the official store, learn how promo codes actually discount your purchase, and see the legal free options.
intro: Buying Excel from an official channel protects you from expired third-party keys and marketplace markups. This guide covers comparing one-time purchases against subscription plans, applying a legitimate promo code at checkout, and understanding which free options are actually legal, since no code gets you a full retail license for nothing.

steps.heading: Compare your license options
steps.body: Weigh the one-time Office Home purchase against a Microsoft 365 Personal or Family subscription to find your best fit.

steps.heading: Add Excel to your cart
steps.body: Select the standalone Excel option and click Buy Now to move it into your cart.

steps.heading: Sign in to your Microsoft account
steps.body: Log in with your Microsoft account email so the purchase links to your profile.

steps.heading: Reach checkout
steps.body: Advance to the checkout screen where your payment options appear.

steps.heading: Apply a promo code
steps.body: Click "Add a promo code," enter a valid discount code, and click Apply to see the reduced total.

steps.heading: Complete the order and activate
steps.body: Place your order, then download and install the software, signing in to verify your license activates correctly.

faq.question: Can a promo code get me a completely free Excel license?
faq.answer: No, any offer promising a full retail license for free using just a promo code is a scam; legitimate codes only provide a percentage discount.

faq.question: Are there any legal ways to use Excel for free?
faq.answer: Yes, the web-based version of Excel is free with a basic Microsoft account, and verified students or educators can often get free academic desktop access.

faq.question: How much can a legitimate promo code actually save me?
faq.answer: Verified partner or seasonal promo codes commonly range from around 10% to 30% off your subscription or retail price.

faq.question: Should I trust a website offering a lifetime product key for a few dollars?
faq.answer: No, unauthenticated third-party keys are a common scam and often stop working, so purchase directly through Microsoft's official store.
